url_verification_summary.competitor_section_strategy: session-side WebSearch at write time per §16.31 Wave 8 + Wave 9 5/5 dead-rate pattern + Wave 6/7 §16.43 confirmation — Stage 2 did not invent plausible firm URLs; RUN session sources fresh via Google Search at write time and applies §16.36 statutory-citation cross-check per page
url_verification_summary.stage2_webfetch_strategy: Stage 2 surfaced authority URLs from Stage 1 seed citations + cross-referenced §33 + §33.1-§33.5 (CIS architecture full lock at MW2 Stage 1b sign-off 2026-05-27) + §31.1-§31.10 + §31.B (Commonhold White Paper 2025 + forthcoming Bill live-pipeline floor — MW3 Stage 1b F-103 lock 2026-05-27) + §26.2 (BSA 2022 leaseholder protections — pre-locked) + §29 + §35.2 + §27.2 + §27.3 + §11.A + §28 + §25.12 + §1 + §5 + §15 + §21 + §23 house position locks; per-page legislation.gov.uk + gov.uk + HMRC manual + BAILII + parliament.uk verification deferred to RUN session under §16.35 per-write discipline. CIS pick (pick 1): SI 2005/2045 reg 4(1) + reg 4(2) (14-day CIS300 deadline) + reg 4(8) (PDS prescribed contents + 14-day deadline + reg 4(9) electronic delivery permission) + FA 2004 s.70 (periodic returns + nil-return s.70(1)(d)) verbatim verified at legislation.gov.uk on 2026-05-27 at seed time. FA 2004 ss.61(2) + s.62 + s.64 + s.66 + s.69 + FA 2021 deemed-contractor £3m rolling-12-month threshold + FA 2009 Sch 55 + Sch 56 + FA 2007 Sch 24 + SI 2019/892 VAT reverse charge + VATA 1994 s.55A + MLR 2017 + ECCTA 2023 verified against §33 + §29 + §27 + §11.A cluster locks. Ready Mixed Concrete [1968] 2 QB 497 employment-vs-self-employment three-element test confirmed against §35.2. Commonhold pick (pick 2): CLRA 2002 Part 1 (ss.1-70 commonhold framework) + Part 2 (ss.71-179 leasehold reform — substantially amended by LFRA 2024 with ss.121-124, 126-128, 130, 132, 134-136, 152-154, 160(4)(d) omitted + ss.167, 172, 178 amended + Schedule 11 paras 4A-4B, 5B-5C inserted + BSA 2022 amendments adding s.38A) verbatim verified at https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2002/15/contents on 2026-05-27 at seed time. LFRA 2024 Part 2 phased commencement + LRGRA 2022 in-force 30 June 2022 + BSA 2022 ss.116-125 + Sch 8 + SI 2024/1018 + LRHUDA 1993 Ch II + LRA 1967 + LTA 1985 ss.18-30 + LPA 1925 s.146 + FA 2003 + TCGA 1992 s.22 + IHTA 1984 verified against §31.1-§31.10 + §31.B + §26.2 + §1 + §5 + §15 cluster locks. Commonhold White Paper March 2025 verified live at gov.uk on 2026-05-27 at seed time.

discoveries_logged: D-1 STAGE2_INHERITED_VERIFICATION — Stage 1 author had already WebFetched + verified SI 2005/2045 reg 4 + FA 2004 s.70 (pick 1) + CLRA 2002 contents + Commonhold White Paper at gov.uk (pick 2) at seed time on 2026-05-27. Stage 2 statutory-citation cross-check confirmed alignment with §33 cluster full lock (pick 1) + §31.1-§31.10 + §31.B (MW3 Stage 1b F-103 lock 2026-05-27 — RESOLVED) + §26.2 (pick 2); no new drift catches at Stage 2 review.

D-2 STAGE2_CONTENT_DEPTH — the 2 Stage 1 seed briefs in this batch are unusually deep at the framing-differentiator + statutory-anchor + key-questions layers (continuing the pattern observed across M3-A-B1/A-B2/B-B1/C-B1/C-B2/C-B3 D-2). Each extended brief landed at +208 to +249 lines of additional content (worked examples + 10-12 FAQs + universal rules + 19-step workflow + Stage 2 author entry). This pattern across the full MW3 sequence confirms that Stage 1 quality compounds Stage 2 efficiency.

D-3 STAGE2_F103_CLOSED_UPSTREAM — F-103 (HOUSE_POSITION_EXTENSION proposing §31.B 'Commonhold White Paper 2025 + Commonhold and Leasehold Reform Bill (forthcoming) — live pipeline floor for future pages') was raised by Stage 1 author + RESOLVED at MW3 Stage 1b sign-off 2026-05-27 BEFORE this Stage 2 batch opened. §31.B HP-lock now operative at house_positions.md line 3816. Pick 2 (commonhold-and-leasehold-reform-bill) inherits the resolution + cross-references §31.B as a load-bearing anchor for the live-pipeline framing. No new HP locks raised at Stage 2.

D-4 STAGE2_LIVE_PIPELINE_VERIFICATION_GATE — pick 2 is the first LIVE-PIPELINE page in MW3 (the Commonhold and Leasehold Reform Bill is not yet introduced to Parliament as of seed-write 2026-05-27; the White Paper March 2025 is the policy basis). Stage 2 brief bakes §16.35 STRICT GATE for Bill-status verification at write time into universal rules + 19-step workflow + opening voice discipline (date-of-write stamp mandatory). The status-distinction discipline (announced / White Paper / Bill / enacted / in force) is named as the load-bearing voice rule. RUN session re-verifies Bill state at parliament.uk + Hansard + gov.uk at write per §16.35 STRICT GATE.

D-5 STAGE2_CLUSTER_LABEL_DRIFT_PARTIAL — pick 2 (commonhold-and-leasehold-reform-bill) queue cluster label 'Service charges and ground rent' is PARTIAL — the slug covers service charges + ground rent BUT ALSO covers commonhold conversion + leasehold reform pipeline. Stage 2 recommends re-cluster to 'Leasehold + commonhold reform pipeline' for cluster-integrity. Cluster routing to property-types-and-specialist-tax category remains correct (substantive specialist-tax angle on commonhold conversion + service-charge + ground-rent tax treatment). Parallel to M3-C-B2 D-6 C10 bed-and-breakfasting drift + M3-C-B3 D-7 triple drift on 'Property accountant for cities / regions'. Administrative not blocking; informational discovery only.

D-6 STAGE2_BUG7_AUTONOMY_CONFIRMED — Bug #7 fix (work autonomously after acknowledgment line) worked cleanly across the 2 picks. No mid-batch pause; no Q-N raised; no blocker. Wall-clock matches the M3-C-B1/B2/B3 patterns (~30-40 minutes for 2 substantive brief extensions on main branch with per-pick commit + final batch-done marker).

D-7 STAGE2_§33_LOCK_PROPAGATED_VIA_TEMPLATES_ANGLE — §33 CIS architecture full lock at MW2 Stage 1b sign-off 2026-05-27 was tested for the second time in MW3 through pick 1 (cis-templates). Where M3-C-B3 pick 6 (cis-deduction-by-contractors-in-the-uk) exercised the deduction-mechanic angle of §33, this batch's pick 1 exercises the TEMPLATES + FIELD-BY-FIELD walkthrough angle (CIS300 + PDS + verification + invoice with labour/materials split + on-boarding compliance). All five §33 do-not-write items + the FA 2021 deemed-contractor £3m threshold + the FA 2026 secondary Class 1 NIC 15% rate + the SI 2019/892 VAT reverse charge in force 1 March 2021 + the Ready Mixed Concrete [1968] employment-status three-element test + reg 4(8) PDS prescribed contents + reg 4(9) electronic delivery permission + reg 4(8)(g) materials-cost direct-cost-to-third-party definition all surfaced cleanly. §33 cluster lock fully operational for property-business CIS pages from multiple angles. Companion-page complete: deduction-mechanic (C18 from M3-C-B3) + verification beginner walkthrough (MW2 B4) + templates-and-field-by-field walkthrough (pick 1 this batch). All three §33-anchored; complementary not cannibalising.

D-8 STAGE2_§31.B_FIRST_TEST — §31.B Commonhold White Paper 2025 + forthcoming Commonhold and Leasehold Reform Bill — MW3 Stage 1b F-103 lock (2026-05-27) is exercised for the first time in MW3 through pick 2 (commonhold-and-leasehold-reform-bill). All eight live-pipeline do-not-write items + the four-part structure (in-force / in-pipeline / investment-consequences / live-status) + the status-distinction discipline (announced / White Paper / Bill / enacted / in force) + the §16.35 STRICT GATE Bill-status verification commitment all surfaced cleanly. The §31.B lock's per-write verification gate framing is the operative voice rule; sessions writing on this cluster MUST re-fetch the live Bill state at write time and update the framing accordingly. If the Bill is introduced to Parliament during writing, the page framing updates to reflect Bill status; if enacted, the page migrates from §31.B floor to a new §31.C enacted-state lock.
anti_templating_check: Two briefs read as a set. Boilerplate sections (Voice + style, 19-step workflow, Stage 2 work log frame) are deliberately verbatim per §4.8 and §7. Substantive content (framing differentiators carried from Stage 1, worked examples, FAQ phrasing, anti-templating notes per pick) is distinct per slug. Each anti-templating note explicitly identifies sibling-collapse risk: pick 1 cis-templates (vs generic construction-industry template-list template OR accountancy-firm-marketing template-thumbnail-behind-lead-form template; statute-anchored five-template field-by-field walkthrough + property-business specificity is the differentiator; field-by-field tables are LOAD-BEARING for the page structure; all five template-specific do-not-write items + the five §33 do-not-write items BANNED framings); pick 2 commonhold-and-leasehold-reform-bill (vs consumer-leaseholder-rights primer template OR solicitor-firm legal-services-led template; investment-impact framing + four-part structure (in-force / in-pipeline / investment-consequences / live-status) + live-pipeline + date-of-write stamp is the differentiator; status-distinction discipline (announced / White Paper / Bill / enacted / in force) is load-bearing voice rule; all eight live-pipeline do-not-write items BANNED framings). No same-sentence drift detected across the set; worked-example personas vary per pick (Patel Developments Ltd mainstream contractor / Patel Holdings Ltd deemed contractor at £3m / P&S Electrical Ltd registered for deduction / NW Builders Ltd gross-payment / M Singh sole-trader unverified / Patel Developments Ltd 12-week Ready Mixed Concrete risk — pick 1; Patel Holdings Ltd 12-flat block freeholder / Ms Singh 65-year-unexpired-term leaseholder / Mr Singh 6-property mixed-tenure BTL / BlockMan Property Services Ltd 25-block managing-agent — pick 2; surnames recur as anonymisation pool not unique-per-pick discipline per program convention). Tables are LOAD-BEARING for both picks (per-template field-by-field walkthroughs for pick 1; in-force-vs-in-pipeline status matrix for pick 2) — sessions MUST NOT collapse to prose.
